minSerializerVersionNote: ENGINE FLOOR (base >= 2.4.0). The base carries render-time permissions IN THE LAYER YAML on pages, grid rows, AND paragraphs (the per-role Customer Center dashboard). Serializer engines <= 0.7.1-beta SILENTLY DROP the grid-row/paragraph permission blocks on deserialize (IgnoreUnmatchedProperties) -> the per-role tiles render UNGATED, a security regression for the consumer. Consume this base ONLY with Truvio.Commerce.Serializer >= 0.8.0-beta, which also carries the deferred permission re-apply pass (+ user-group cache refresh) that lands group grants instead of collapsing to the Anonymous=None safety fallback when groups deserialize after content. Verify post-deserialize with the permissions-parity gate leg (every serialized block <-> matching UnifiedPermission rows).
description: Machine-readable guarantees the base layer makes to every addition (v3.0 Distribution). Additions (catalog/feature/sample-data/surface/theme layers) bind ONLY to this contract - never to each other. Consolidates the ID-range/collision rules, the seeded anchors from Invoke-SeedGating.ps1 / Invoke-SeedVerify.ps1, and the anchor areas/repositories from config/gate-config.json + swift-2.3.json. The gate reads this file for the base-contract collision check. Operator-confirmed 2026-07-05 (DISTRIBUTION-DECISIONS.md).

idRules.nvarcharPkNote: When an addition writes rows into a base-owned table whose PK is nvarchar (most Ecom tables - PriceId, PaymentId, ProductId, etc.), it MUST namespace the PK with a PACK-<NAME>- prefix (uppercase layer name). Keeps additions disjoint from the base and from each other.
idRules.intIdentityNote: When an addition writes rows keyed by an int-identity PK, it MUST reserve an id at or above the 100000 floor. Applies equally to item-instance ids (ItemType_<systemName> PK rows).
idRules.baseCatalogNote: The base ships ZERO catalog (scaffolding-only, swift/2.3.2): EcomGroups, EcomProducts, EcomGroupProductRelation, EcomVariantGroups, EcomVariantsOptions, EcomVariantOptionsProductRelation, EcomDiscount, EcomDiscountTranslation, EcomPrices carry no base rows. The collision surface for these tables is addition-vs-fixture-vs-addition, arbitrated statically by the gate.
idRules.reservedFixtureNote: The gate fixture catalog (catalog-kind layer) reserves these key prefixes. Additions must not use them.
idRules.noPerItemTypeNumericTableNote: The contract is prefix-based, not per-itemType-numeric. There is deliberately NO per-itemType numeric range table - do not invent one.

guaranteedRows.permissionGroupsNote: DW 10.26.9 has no AccessUserGroup table - groups ARE AccessUser rows of type 2.

referenceCategory.note: Hidden template category (CategoryType 2) required by DemoVerifier Check 2. Seeded pre-host-start (DW caches at startup).

contractPrice.note: Seeded by Invoke-SeedGating; SKIPPED under -SkipContractRow (the base baseline ships the row itself).

repositories.name: Products
repositories.note: The base guarantees the Products repository/index/query/facets are provisioned by the gate. 'ProductsFrontend' is dead/removed - never provisioned by the clean-room.

replaceFiltered.note: FILTER-01: only the three permission groups are base-owned on AccessUser; user rows (1328/1326) are seeded, not serialized into the base.

baseOwnedTables.note: Additions touching a base-owned table follow idRules (prefix / floor) and must pass the static intersection checks below. Base ships zero catalog (see idRules.baseCatalog).

perEnvironmentAreaExclusions.note: Per-environment Area configuration deliberately NOT serialized by the base (LRN-08). These Area columns/fields are excluded by config/swift-2.3.json (the 'Site framework' deploy predicate's excludeAreaColumns + excludeFields), so the base ships brand/content-neutral structure only and every consuming environment owns these values. A fresh deserialize therefore leaves them at whatever the target DB already holds - it does NOT overwrite them.

consumerObligation.bindSiteRoot: After deserialize, set each area's AreaFrontpage to the environment's site-root page id.
consumerObligation.bindShop: After deserialize, set each area's AreaEcomShopId to the environment shop. The base ships exactly one shop: SHOP1 ('B2B Commerce Store').
consumerObligation.restart: Restart the DW host after binding - Area routing, frontpage resolution, and shop resolution are cached at host startup.

protectedItemTypes.note: Upstream DW/Swift identifiers - NEVER renamed by the vocabulary rewrite (plan section 3.1). PK tables are ItemType_<systemName> (hyphenated on DW 10.26.9).

collisionChecks.rule: Intersect an addition's _sql/<Table>/*.yml filenames (= row keys; composite keys join with $$) with the base's filenames for the same table. Non-empty = FAIL.
WR-10: the same table+filename shipped by >1 active addition = FAIL. A single layer shipping the same key in its own replace+merge is NOT a collision.

07-01: an addition's fragment-shipped _content relative paths must be disjoint from base _content paths (union of both mode trees); cross-layer parity; shipped-files-only (referenced base anchors excluded); declared-footprint parity (every Content entry matches a declared fragmentContent {areaId, path}).

WR-04: (itemType, fields.Id) pairs land as PK rows in shared ItemType_<systemName> tables. The same pair by >1 owner = FAIL. Reusing an id NUMBER across DIFFERENT itemType tables is fine (PK surface is per-table).

registrationAsymmetry.rule: Every csLedger entry with registration 'addin-attribute-config-row' MUST have >=1 matching configRows entry.
registrationAsymmetry.note: Cross-field rule enforced at activation (not JSON-Schema-expressible); locked by tests.
